title: Deployment-time initializer runs before proxy admin is set, enabling privilege capture by the deployer or factory
claim: `AdminUpgradeabilityProxy` invokes the base `UpgradeabilityProxy` constructor first, and that constructor immediately `delegatecall`s `_data` into the implementation before `_setAdmin(_admin)` runs. Any initializer that derives ownership or privileged roles from `msg.sender` will therefore assign them to the deploying EOA/factory rather than to the intended proxy admin.
impact: A malicious or compromised deployer/factory can come out of deployment holding the proxied system's privileged roles, allowing theft, governance capture, hostile reconfiguration, or permanent denial of administrative recovery even though a different `_admin` address was supplied.
paths: A factory or deployer creates `AdminUpgradeabilityProxy` with non-empty initialization calldata.
The implementation initializer uses a common pattern such as `owner = msg.sender` or granting an admin role to `msg.sender`.
Because the delegatecall executes before `ADMIN_SLOT` is populated, the deployer/factory receives those privileges instead of the intended admin.

title: `upgradeToAndCall` can strand privileged roles on a non-forwarding transparent-proxy admin
claim: During `upgradeToAndCall`, the initializer executes via `delegatecall` with `msg.sender == admin`. Transparent-proxy fallback then forbids that same admin address from calling implementation functions. If the admin is a non-forwarding contract such as a `ProxyAdmin`-style helper and the initializer assigns owner/governor/pauser roles to `msg.sender`, those roles can be trapped on an address that cannot exercise them through the proxy.
impact: Critical operational permissions such as pause, rescue, or ownership-controlled recovery can become unusable after an upgrade, turning otherwise recoverable incidents into prolonged or permanent lockups until a migration is possible, if one remains possible at all.
paths: The proxy admin is a contract that can perform proxy admin actions but cannot generically call arbitrary implementation functions.
An upgrade uses `upgradeToAndCall(newImplementation, initData)` where the initializer sets a privileged role to `msg.sender`.
The role is assigned to the admin contract address, but that address is blocked by `_willFallback()` from invoking the proxied role-gated functions.

title: Constructor accepts `address(0)` as admin, permanently disabling upgrades and admin recovery
claim: The constructor writes `_admin` directly into `ADMIN_SLOT` without validating that it is nonzero, even though `changeAdmin` later explicitly rejects the zero address. A deployment mistake can therefore install `address(0)` as the admin forever.
impact: If the proxy is deployed with a zero admin, no caller can execute `changeAdmin`, `upgradeTo`, or `upgradeToAndCall`. Any future need for an emergency upgrade, migration, or asset-recovery action becomes impossible, which can convert otherwise recoverable bugs into permanent loss or lockup.
paths: Deployment tooling or a factory passes `_admin = address(0)`.
The constructor stores the zero address in `ADMIN_SLOT`.
All admin-only entry points become permanently unreachable because no transaction can originate from `address(0)`.

title: Proxy never revalidates implementation code at call time, so a code-less implementation address can turn calls into silent no-ops
claim: The proxy checks `Address.isContract(newImplementation)` only when storing the implementation address. At runtime, `_fallback()` blindly `delegatecall`s whatever address is stored in `IMPLEMENTATION_SLOT`. If that address later ends up with no runtime code on the target chain, the delegatecall can succeed without executing any logic, causing apparent success with no state transition.
impact: User and integration calls can stop performing the intended logic while still returning successfully, which can freeze assets, break accounting invariants, and mislead callers into believing state-changing operations succeeded.
paths: The proxy is pointed at an implementation address that later loses its runtime code or is otherwise left code-less on the deployment chain.
Users continue interacting with the proxy through fallback.
`_delegate(_implementation())` executes against an address with no code, and calls return without running the intended implementation logic.
